entry.title: Production supply to next PSA in Cloud ERP
entry.content:
In many production environments, semifinished products are used immediately as components for a subsequent production step. Instead of putting these materials away into the warehouse after production, they can be supplied directly to the next production line upon goods receipt.

With Warehouse Production Integration with Synchronous Goods Receipt (Scope Item 5HO), SAP Cloud ERP enables exactly this scenario:
Warehouse tasks are created automatically during goods receipt, ensuring a seamless and efficient material flow from production to the next production supply area (PSA).

Why Use This Scenario?

Implementing this process offers several key benefits:

  • Reduced effort for end users, as fewer manual system steps are required

  • Elimination of errors caused by manual warehouse task creation
  • Faster processing due to reduced physical movements
  • A lean and streamlined production supply process

Overall, this approach significantly improves efficiency and transparency in production logistics.

 

Relevant scope item: Warehouse Production Integration with Synchronous Goods Receipt (5HO)

 

Configuration Overview

To enable this scenario, the following configuration steps and master data settings are required:

  • Activation of synchronous goods movements in the production scheduling profile

  • Maintenance of target PSA, destination bin, and warehouse in the production version of the product
  • Setup of automatic warehouse task creation upon goods receipt using the warehouse process type determination indicator in the warehouse product
  • Configuration of SSCUI 104392 – Determine Warehouse Process Type
  • Maintenance of a control cycle for the target PSA, including the destination bin

All these elements work together to ensure that the warehouse task is created automatically and the material is routed correctly.

Configuration:

  • Activation of synchronous goods movements in production scheduling profile

     

    • Target PSA and Destination bin and warehouse maintained in the production version of the product
  • Setup of automatic warehouse task creation upon receipt using warehouse process type determination indicator in warehouse product and setup
    • SSCUI 104392 – Determine Warehouse Process Type
  • Control Cycle for target PSA with destination bin

 

Example Scenario:

1. Step: Create Production Order

  • Material: SG424
  • Production Order: 1077350
    • Make sure to select the correct Production Scheduling Profile. 
    • Goods Receipt Location must be WM-managed Storage Location

2. Step: Confirm Production Order

  • Click on "Goods Movement Overview"
  • As you can see, there are two entries for goods movement. Goods receipt posting for material SG424 with movement type 101 and goods issue posting for component RM124 with movement type 261. 
  • Scrolling to the right, you will find the corresponding information about EWM Storage Bin, Target PSA and EWM Target Bin.  
  • After posting the goods movements, in the production order you will find the material document.
  • After posting goods receipt, there is an automatic warehouse task created to next production supply area
  • As maintained in the production version, after posting goods receipt, the material will be on PR-AREA. The destination bin is maintained in the control cycle. 

In conclusion, this use case demonstrates how semifinished products can be seamlessly transferred from production directly to the next production supply area without being put away into the warehouse. By automatically creating warehouse tasks upon goods receipt, manual steps are eliminated and the risk of errors during warehouse task creation is significantly reduced.

The article showed how the interaction of the relevant configuration steps and master data—such as the production scheduling profile, production version, control cycle, and warehouse process type determination—is essential to enable this scenario successfully. When these settings are correctly maintained, the synchronous goods receipt process ensures a fast, reliable, and efficient material flow.

As a result, customers can achieve a leaner production supply process, reduce physical movements, save valuable time, and work more efficiently in real-life production environments.

Questions or feedback are always welcome and highly appreciated!

Thanks for reading this blog post. Stay tuned!

Follow the PSCC_Enablement  tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

entry.title: Asset Management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to the blog post for asset management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release! This release introduces significant innovations in maintenance management, designed to enhance productivity for maintenance experts, improve safety and compliance protocols, and provide more actionable, centralized data.

Below, you’ll find a curated selection of release highlights from our core product management team. Before diving in, I invite you to watch the release highlights video, featuring a digital version of me! 😀

Table of Contents

New Asset Overview SAP Fiori app (+demo) 
Permit to work: new scope item (7Z4) (+demo) 
Enhancements to the Manage Maintenance Notification app (+demo) 
Further innovations 
SAP Asset Performance Management 
More information/additional links

New Asset Overview SAP Fiori app

To address the challenge of navigating multiple screens to gather asset information, the 2602 release introduces the Asset Overview app. This SAP Fiori application provides a single, unified interface for maintenance experts to view and interact with the complete hierarchy of their enterprise assets. Access to this application requires EAM - Asset Overview (SAP_EAM_BC_AOVW_MNG_PC) business catalog, which is included in role templates such as Maintenance Planner (SAP_BR_MAINTENANCE_PLANNER), Maintenance Technician (SAP_BR_MAINTENANCE_TECHNICIAN), and Maintenance Supervisor (SAP_BR_MAINTENANCE_SUPERVISOR).

Key features and capabilities:

Unified hierarchy visualization: The app offers a comprehensive top-down and bottom-up view of your asset structure, combining functional locations and the equipment installed within them into a single, traversable tree. Users can expand individual nodes or the entire hierarchy to its lowest level with a single click. A powerful search function allows users to isolate a specific asset and display its complete branch up to the top-level parent.

The new app aims to provide asset-centric data access, focused on:

  • Flexible column layout: This feature allows a user to select an asset in the hierarchy on the left-hand panel and view its detailed information on the right-hand panel without leaving the main screen.
  • Master and transactional data: The detail view displays crucial master data, such as manufacturer and model number, alongside visual indicators for warranty status (e.g., color-coded for active, expired or not maintained). It also provides direct access to related transactional data, including open maintenance notifications and orders associated with the asset.

Integrated maintenance actions: The app is not just for viewing data; it enables immediate action. Users can create maintenance notifications directly from the asset detail page through two methods:

  • Quick create: A simple form requiring a description to generate a notification.
  • Joule, SAP’s AI Copilot: Users can leverage the integrated digital assistant, Joule, to create a notification using natural language prompts without leaving the app.

Learn more watching the demo below:

 

Permit to work: new scope item (7Z4)

The 2602 release introduces the Permit to Work functionality under scope item 7Z4. This feature provides a standardized process for issuing permits for high-risk maintenance activities, ensuring work is performed in a safe, secure, and compliant manner. It is particularly critical for industries such as manufacturing, oil and gas, mining, and utilities.

At its core, the Permit to Work process serves as a formal authorization system. It's designed to foster close collaboration between your maintenance, operations, and safety teams, ensuring everyone is aligned on the required precautions to minimize risks.

Initially, this scope item is available for Germany and the US. Availability for other countries will follow in subsequent updates, with a target for all standard countries by the 2608 release.

Prerequisite scope items include Reactive Maintenance (4HH), Proactive Maintenance (4HI), Operational and Overhead Maintenance (4WM) and Improvement maintenance (4VT).

Core components and configuration

New applications: This functionality is delivered with two new SAP Fiori apps:

  • Manage Work Permit Template (F4692): Allows for the creation of reusable templates with pre-defined information. This reduces the time required to create new permits and ensures that minimum safety and data standards are consistently met.
  • Manage Work Permits (F6579): The primary application for creating, processing, and managing the lifecycle of individual work permits. Permits can be created with reference to a maintenance order, an existing permit, or a pre-configured template.

Process overview

1. Initiation: A planner identifies a maintenance activity that requires a permit and initiates its creation, selecting a permit type such as hot work, cold work, or confined space.
2. Approval: The permit is submitted for review. In the initial 2602 release, the permit is automatically set to a "Ready for Issue" status. Flexible workflow capabilities are planned for future releases.
3. Issuance: An authorized user, such as a supervisor, issues the permit to the designated technician.
4. Execution and return: The technician completes the work and returns the permit to the issuer.
5. Closure: The issuer verifies the work site, normalizes the technical object, and formally closes the permit in the system. The system supports actions to "return and close" or "return and deactivate" the permit if work needs to be suspended and resumed later.

 Learn more watching the demo below by Ebin Joseph, from product management:

 

Enhancements to the Manage Maintenance Notification app

The Manage Maintenance Notification (F5777) app has been updated with several new features to improve usability and provide deeper insights.

Situation handling: The app now uses situation handling to deliver instant alerts to the maintenance team when a new notification is created or a key status is updated. For example, an alert can be triggered when a notification is automatically generated due to a condition-based maintenance threshold breach, enabling a proactive response.

Advanced filtering: The list report now includes "Asset Location" and "Sort Field" as filter criteria. This allows users to more effectively narrow down the list of notifications to the specific assets or areas for which they are responsible.

Visual charts for measurement documents: For notifications generated from a measurement document threshold breach, the header now includes a chart. This provides a clear visual representation of the measurement readings against their defined thresholds, allowing for quicker analysis.

Reference order tracking: A "Reference Order" field has been added as a filter and a column in the list report. This enables maintenance teams to easily identify and track all notifications that were created as a follow-on work request from a technician in the field.

Conversational AI: Similar to the Asset Overview app, users can now create maintenance notifications directly within this app using natural language conversations with Joule AI Copilot.

 Learn more watching the demo below by Syed Kader Abdul Wahab, from product management:

 

Further innovations

Perform Maintenance Jobs app - Long text formatting: Clarity in work instructions is critical for maintenance technicians. Long text fields for maintenance orders and jobs can now be formatted. This enhancement allows for the use of bolding, bullet points, and other formatting options to structure text, which improves the readability and organization of detailed instructions and notes.

Manage Maintenance Plans app - Deletion of sort fields: To improve data quality and efficiency, the Define Sort Fields for Maintenance Plans configuration activity now includes a delete option. You can now remove outdated or invalid customized sort field values. This ensures that only accurate and relevant sort field values are available in the Maintenance Plan application, improving accuracy in plan management.

Centralized processing of maintenance order workflows in SAP Task Center: Efficiency in approval processes is a key driver of productivity. Maintenance Order workflow attributes are now accessible in SAP Task Center, allowing for centralized processing of approvals. The following attributes are included: Order Description, Order Type, Planner Group, Planning Plant, Basic End Date, Basic Start Date, Priority, and Technical Object. This centralization helps managers and approvers process tasks from a single, unified inbox.

SAP Asset Performance Management 

SAP Asset Performance Management integrates with SAP Cloud ERP and empowers asset owners, plant managers, and reliability engineers to optimize maintenance strategies, measure and improve asset performance, and follow through on your sustainability objectives. The 2601 release highlights include bulk activation and deactivation of system elements, more flexible risk and criticality assessment outcomes, CAP-based reliability assessment applications with extensibility, configurable RCM consequence questionnaires, validity periods for assessments, time-window–based rule processing, and embedded analytics dashboards. Read the complete blog post by Kate Dowle: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2601.

If you’d like to stay up to date with the latest APM innovations, follow my colleague Kate Dowle who regularly shares updates and new blog posts on the topic.
 

More information / additional links 

Find all enablement assets and update blog post for Asset Management: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management – The Link Collection

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Also, visit the Asset Management community page. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up-to-date to our latest enablement assets: Fernanda Rodrigues | LinkedIn

 

 Find all our assets

Do you want to learn more about the main innovations in different lines of business in the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release? The release 2602 showcases more 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

Enablement

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you: 

 

 ️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series!

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Asset Management as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.

entry.title: Build a Winning IT Operating Model for Your S/4HANA Investment
entry.content:
Werde zum Value Partner: Entfessele das volle Potenzial der S/4HANA-Investition mit dem richtigen IT Operating Model!

Die digitale Transformation ist immer noch für viele Unternehmen ein zentrales strategisches Ziel – und SAP S/4HANA spielt dabei in vielen Unternehmen eine Schlüsselrolle. Ein maßgeschneidertes IT Operating Model (ITOM) ist die Voraussetzung dafür, dass die Potenziale von S/4HANA voll ausgeschöpft werden können. Dieser Beitrag zeigt, worauf es beim Aufbau eines ITOM im SAP-Kontext ankommt – und welche strategischen Entscheidungen dabei zu treffen sind.​ 

Ausgangslage 

Ein wirksames ITOM entsteht nicht im luftleeren Raum. Vielmehr hängt es entscheidend vom Reifegrad der bestehenden SAP-Landschaft und Organisation ab:​ 

  • SAP bereits umfänglich im Einsatz: Wenn SAP bereits tief in der Organisation verankert ist, existieren in der Regel klare Verantwortlichkeiten, Prozesse und ein grundlegendes Verständnis für die SAP-Anwendungen und Technologien.​ 
  • SAP als Nischenlösung: Wird SAP nur in einzelnen Bereichen z.B. in der Buchhaltung eingesetzt, sind die Erfahrungen mit SAP zu arbeiten mit etwas Geschick auch auf andere Bereiche z.B. Produktionsplanung, Logistik übertragbar. Schließlich weiß die Organisation bereits, wie SAP "tickt". 
  • SAP als Neuland: Hier muss sich erst ein breites Verständnis für die SAP-Anwendungen und Technologien entwickeln im Projektverlauf und nach dem go-live im Betrieb. In Unternehmen ohne SAP-Vorerfahrung ist zusätzlich in Change-Management, Qualifizierungsmaßnahmen und kulturelle Integration zu investieren.​ 

Je nach Ausgangssituation müssen IT-Verantwortliche ihr ITOM gezielt ausrichten – sowohl organisatorisch als auch kulturell. Der enge Schulterschluss mit den Fachbereichen ist essenziell. In allen drei Ausgangssituationen hat die S/4 HANA-Einführung eine Vielzahl von Implikationen z.B. auf die Governance,  auf die Skalierbarkeit, auf den Einsatz neuer Technologien, Arbeitsweisen und Prozesse.​ 

Kritische Erfolgsdimensionen für das ITOM​ 

Beim Design des Operating Models müssen verschiedene Schlüsselbereiche berücksichtigt werden. Viele Darstellungen beschränken sich auf die 3 Dimensionen Prozesse, Technologie und Mensch. Erfahrungsgemäß lohnt sich eine genauere Aufteilung der Schlüsselbereiche 

  • Architektur: Häufig bereits zu Projektbeginn definiert, bildet sie eine feste Rahmenbedingung – sollte aber regelmäßig auf strategische Passfähigkeit geprüft werden.​ 
  • Tooling & IT-Prozesse: Von DevOps über Monitoring bis hin zu Support-Prozessen – effiziente Toolchain und klar definierte Prozesse z.B. nach ITIL sind unerlässlich.​ 
  • Organisationsstruktur: Ob zentralisiert, dezentral oder hybrid – die Struktur muss zur operativen Realität passen.​ 
  • Prozessansatz: Funktionale oder End-to-End-Ausrichtung oder beides? Der gewählte Ansatz hat direkte Auswirkungen auf Verantwortlichkeiten und Schnittstellen.​ 
  • Way of Working: Agile, klassische oder hybride Arbeitsweisen beeinflussen das Design der Rollen und Aufgabenverteilung.​ 
  • Change Management, Ausbildung, Kompetenz: Mit Unsicherheiten und Skepsis in der Belegschaft sollte offensiv umgegangen werden. Eine fokussierte Change-Begleitung ist sehr zu empfehlen für den Gesamterfolg der Transformation. 

Nicht alle dieser Bereiche sind frei gestaltbar – viele sind durch bestehende IT-Strategien oder Unternehmensvorgaben determiniert. Daher ist es essenziell, zu Beginn des ITOM-Designs zwischen fixen Rahmenbedingungen und flexibel gestaltbaren Elementen zu unterscheiden. Mit einer S/4HANA Transformation besteht die unbedingte Möglichkeit, Strukturen zu hinterfragen und "alte Zöpfe" neu zu ordnen.​ 

Wie sich die verschiedenen Schlüsselbereiche in den Projekt- und Betriebskontext einordnen lassen und welche Aufgaben der/die ITOM-Verantwortliche währende die S/4 HANA Einführung hat, zeigt die folgende Tabelle in einer Übersicht. 

 Schlüsselbereiche für den CCOE Aufbau

Strukturierter Aufbau: Vorgehensmodell zur ITOM-Entwicklung​ 

Folgende Schritte haben sich in der Praxis bewährt:​ 

1. Benennung eines verantwortlichen ITOM Leads:  

  • Sicherstellung klarer Verantwortlichkeiten für Design und Umsetzung des Operating Models. 
  • Verankerung der Rolle auf ausreichender Hierarchieebene (z. B. direkt unterhalb der CIO-/IT-Leitung).​ 
  • Aufbau eines schlagkräftigen Kernteams mit Vertretern aus relevanten Domänen (z. B. Architektur, Betrieb, Prozesse).​ 

2. Definition von Zielbild, Prioritäten und Vision​ 

  • Erarbeitung eines klaren Zielbilds für das zukünftige ITOM: Rolle, Beitrag zur Gesamtstrategie, Abgrenzung.​ 
  • Festlegung von Leitzielen wie z. B. Skalierbarkeit, Automatisierung oder Business-Alignment.​ 
  • Identifikation und Priorisierung der größten Hebel und Handlungsfelder – z.B. auch die Etablierung neuer “Spielregeln” wie Clean Core, SAP BTP first bei Erweiterungen, durchgängige Tool Chain 

(3. Optional) Bestandsaufnahme des aktuellen TOM (As-Is)​ 

  • Erhebung vorhandener Rollen, Prozesse, Tools und Strukturen (ggf. durch Interviews, Workshops, Dokumentenanalyse).​
  • Bewertung der Reifegrade in den einzelnen ITOM-Dimensionen.​ 
  • Identifikation von Redundanzen, Lücken, Pain Points oder auch Best Practices im aktuellen Setup.​ 

4. Definition zentraler Aufgabenbereiche (Building Blocks siehe Grafik)​ 

  • Clusterung aller ITOM-Aufgabenbereiche nach strategischen, Governance- und operativen Fähigkeiten - z.B. Sourcingstrategie, Business- und IT-Zusammenarbeit, Applikations- und SAP-Basis Betrieb 
  • Orientierung an IT-Prozessen und –Funktionen sowie der Business- und IT Zusammenarbeit. 
  • Klare Abgrenzung der Aufgabenbereiche zur Vermeidung von Verantwortlichkeitslücken.​ 

Aufgabenbereiche im S/4 HANA Betrieb

5. Erste Zuordnung von Rollen zu Aufgabenbereichen (High-Level)​ 

  • Benennung von Rollen pro Aufgabenbereich – z. B. Produkt Owner, Architekt, Applikations-/Prozess-Experte.​ 
  • Berücksichtigung vorhandener Rollen in der Organisation zur Vermeidung von Doppelstrukturen.​ 
  • Differenzierung zwischen Rollen und Stellen. 

6. Detaillierung von Rollen und Verantwortlichkeiten​ 

  • Detaillierung der Aufgabenbereiche und Erstellung eines RACI-Modells zur klaren Aufgabenverteilung (Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed).​ 
  • Beschreibung der Rollenprofile inklusive benötigter Kompetenzen, KPIs und Zielbilder.​ 
  • Validierung der Rollenzuordnung mit Fachbereichen, Betriebs- und Projektorganisationen. 

7. Identifikation von Lernbedarfen und Kompetenzlücken​ 

  • Ableitung von Trainingspfaden für bestehende Mitarbeitende basierend auf neuem Rollen- und Skillbedarf.​ 
  • Aufbau von Lernformaten - z. B. learning journeys, E-Learnings, SAP Learning Hub.​ 
  • Identifikation von Bedarf an Neueinstellungen oder externem Know-how - z. B. in Architektur, DevOps. 

8. Definition der Zielorganisation und Teamstrukturen​ 

  • Entwicklung eines Organisationsdesigns inkl. Teamgrößen, Berichtslinien und Verantwortlichkeiten - z.B. was gehört in das SAP CCOE und was nicht.
  • Entscheidung über Betriebsmodelle - z. B. zentral vs. dezentral, Inhouse vs. Outsourcing. 
  • Verankerung der ITOM-Strukturen im Operating Model der Gesamt-IT.
  • Organisatorischer Umgang mit neuen Themen z.B. Künstliche Intelligenz 

Ein iterativer, faktenbasierter Ansatz stellt sicher, dass das Operating Model nicht nur auf dem Papier funktioniert, sondern im operativen Alltag tragfähig ist.​ 

​ ...

Fazit​ 

Ein zielgerichtetes Operating Model für SAP S/4HANA ist ein zentraler Erfolgsfaktor für die digitale Transformation der IT-Landschaft. Es ermöglicht nicht nur einen stabilen und skalierbaren Betrieb der SAP-Plattform, sondern schafft auch die Grundlage für Innovation und Agilität. IT-Verantwortliche sollten die Gestaltung des ITOM daher als strategische Aufgabe begreifen – mit klarer Vision, strukturierter Umsetzung und konsequenter Verankerung in der Organisation. 

 ...

Torsten Scheffler ist selbständiger Berater und hat über 200 CCOE Projekte durchgeführt. 

...

Useful links: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-architecture-blog-posts/building-high-performing-sap-it-organization-the-art-of-organizational/ba-p/13464999

entry.title: Efficient Document Inbound Processing in SAP Cloud ERP with SAP Document AI: March 12, 2026
entry.content:
Managing incoming documents efficiently is critical for business operations. SAP Cloud ERP, powered by SAP Document AI, offers pre-delivered use cases to automate and optimize document processing across Sales, Finance, and Quality Management.

In this live session, we’ll explore how SAP Document AI enhances document inbound processing, reducing manual effort and improving accuracy. You’ll see live demos of end-to-end workflows and learn how to activate these capabilities in your system.

Key Takeaways:

  • Discover pre-built document processing use cases in SAP Cloud ERP.
  • See live demos of end-to-end workflows for Sales, Finance, and Quality Management.
  • Learn how to activate and extend these capabilities for your business needs.

Who Should Attend? Project managers, consultants, business users, and administrators looking to improve document processing efficiency.

Register Now!

entry.title: Announcing: Enhancements to Quality Gates and Embedded Launch Activities for SAP Cloud ERP and HCM
entry.content:
Enhancing Project Success: New CQC Reports and Embedded Launch Activity support for Quality Gates

There's a lot that needs to be done, done correctly, and done at the right time for your SAP solution deployment to go smoothly. SAP created the SAP Activate Methodology to guide project teams through the most essential tasks for successful implementation, but how can you check that you're on track as you go? This is what quality gates are designed to do. Quality gates are periodic checkpoints that focus in on the most essential tasks that should be completed before moving onto the next phase. Multiple teams in SAP have come together in 2025 to improve the quality gates, and in this blog I go through the 3 major improvements to Quality Gates in HCM and SAP Cloud ERP (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition). 

  1. Refreshed Quality Gate Content
  2. Automated Risk Analysis via CQC Reports in SAP Cloud ALM
  3. 1:1 Quality Gate Support available via Embedded Launch Activities

    Quality gate execution is a collaborative milestone, driven by a joint effort between partner and customer project teams and led by their respective project managers. These checkpoints empower teams to demonstrate systematic due diligence and clear, tangible progress toward solution adoption and realized business value.

    See the sections below for details on each of these enhancements.

    1. Refreshed Quality Gate Content

     

    All quality gate questions for all SAP Activate phases have been reviewed and refreshed. Key improvements are:

    1. All checklist questions have been refreshed to be applicable to the latest SAP concepts, products, and assets
    2. Relevant supporting links and assets have been added to the question documentation (e.g. explanatory links of what the Digital Discovery Assessment (DDA) is have been linked in checklist questions that address the DDA)
    3. New supporting assets created to articulate the concept of quality gates, why they're important, and the CQC reports now available (Quality Gate Overview Deck, Quality Gate Execution - Detailed Walkthrough Deck, SAP Activate & Quality Gate Introductory Video)
    4. Language has been harmonized across the business suite - i.e. roles and terms will be more consistent across roadmaps from different solution areas such as HCM, ERP (Public and Private), Concur, SCM, BTP etc.

      The best way to access and review Quality Gate Questions is via SAP Cloud ALM, but you can also view the refreshed list of questions via the SAP Activate Roadmaps accelerators linked here (HCM | SAP Cloud ERP)

      2. Automated Risk Analysis: The CQC Report for Quality Gates

      The biggest hurdle in a Quality Gate isn't just answering the questions - it's knowing what to do when an answer reveals a risk.

      When you complete your Quality Gate checklists directly in SAP Cloud ALM, you can now generate a Continuous Quality Check (CQC) for Implementation Gates report*. This is a tailored advisory document generated within one business day.

      *As of 2026-02, this report is available to request in SAP Cloud ALM for the Prepare and Explore Phase Quality Gates for SAP Cloud ERP and HCM. It will be rolled out for Realize, Deploy and Run in Q2 2026 for SAP Cloud ERP and HCM. It is already available for all phases for SAP Cloud ERP Private.  Availability for other solution areas is under development for 2026.

      What the CQC Report delivers:

      • Management Summary: A high-level view for stakeholders on project readiness.

      • Individualized Action Plan: Trackable tasks in SAP Cloud ALM to course-correct identified risks.

      • Detailed Recommendations: Direct links to SAP accelerators and documentation to help your team resolve gaps.

        By using this self-service report at the end of the Prepare and Explore phases, you mitigate early-stage risks like unvalidated financial structures or incomplete integration designs before they impact your delivery timeline.

        3. Personalized Guidance: Enhanced Embedded Launch Activities

        We know that digital tools are most effective when paired with expert human insight. That’s why we are enhancing our Embedded Launch Activities - the onboarding services many of you already utilize.

        Starting with new customer contracts as of March 1, 2026 we are including 1:1 Onboarding Advisor support for one of your Quality Gate checklists. This means you can have a dedicated SAP expert walk through the checklist with you. The onboarding advisor will be able to support with on the spot clarifications of concepts, resources etc.,  helping the team to ensure that your "Yes" answers are truly backed by best practices and that your "No" answers have a solid mitigation plan in place.

        Why This Matters for Your Project

        Quality Gates occur at every major transition point - from the end of Prepare through to Run (where they repeat every 6 months). They are designed to be "health checks," not performance reviews.

        By utilizing the new CQC reports and Advisor support, you achieve two primary things:

        1. Confidence: That the project team has completed the most essential tasks, is aware of the most essential assets, and knows what you need to know to move forward, or course correct.

        2. Speed: You're more likely to achieve your goals on time by doing it right the first time - and the checklists help to safeguard that.

           

          Next Steps: How to Get Started

          • Access your project in SAP Cloud ALM: Ensure your Quality Gate checklists are being completed digitally rather than in manual spreadsheets to unlock report functionality. Follow this detailed guide to begin utilizing quality gates in SAP Cloud ALM.

          • Request your CQC Report: For SAP Cloud ERP and HCM projects for Prepare & Explore Phases, once the checklist is complete, use the “Request SAP Assessment Service” button. Your report will be available in SAP Cloud ALM and SAP for Me within 24 hours.

          • Book your Advisor Session: For new customer contracts as of March 1st, reach out to your onboarding advisor to schedule your 1:1 session for your chosen Quality Gate milestone.  

            Let us know your thoughts or questions in the comments below!

          •  

          •  

      •  

      •

entry.title: Migration Cockpit in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
I am a product expert for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. I am delighted to share some exciting innovations from our latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 release. This update brings significant enhancements to the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit, designed to make your data migration projects smoother, more transparent, and more efficient. These new features are particularly relevant for data migration experts, project managers, and business teams guiding their organizations through the transition to our intelligent cloud ERP.

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

Migration Cockpit Release 2602 enhances data migration by enabling deeper exploration of migration objects, leveraging staging tables as structured intermediate storage, and introducing transfer lists for better control and transparency during data loads. With increased file size limits, it ensures faster, more efficient handling of large data volumes—reducing complexity, improving reliability, and accelerating system transition projects.

Migration Cockpit

  • Explore Migration Cockpit
  • Using Staging Tables as Intermediate Storage
  • Transfer List
  • Increased File Size Limits for an Efficient Data Load

Explore Migration Objects: A New SAP Fiori App for Enhanced Project Planning

A successful data migration relies on meticulous planning and a deep understanding of data dependencies. With the 2602 release, we are introducing the SAP Fiori app, "Explore Migration Objects," to bring a new level of clarity to your project preparation.

This app provides crucial insights into migration object dependencies and their relationships to specific scope items. It empowers your project team to efficiently plan data migration projects by ensuring that all mandatory and optional predecessor objects are correctly identified and migrated in the proper sequence. Through interactive graphs, you can now visualize how migration objects are interconnected. The app allows for detailed dependency analysis, differentiating between mandatory and optional predecessors, and provides focused search and direct access to migration object documentation. By visualizing these relationships, your team can make informed decisions, ultimately reducing project risks and optimizing timelines. Please note that this initial version of the app currently supports migration objects built for the "Migrate Data Using Staging Tables" approach.

 

 

Enhanced Flexibility with Staging Tables for Direct System Migration

For customers using the "Migrate Data Directly from SAP System" approach, the 2602 release introduces a powerful new capability: the use of staging tables as intermediate storage. This feature is designed to facilitate the adjustment of selected data before it is loaded into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This enhancement significantly increases transparency and provides robust validation capabilities for your selected data. It gives your team more flexibility to adjust and cleanse application data from the source SAP system before the Now, data adaptation is performed within ETL tool prior to loading the data into the migration cockpit’s staging tables. Once the data is prepared and validated, you can transfer the ready-for-processing data to the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit for further processing, ensuring higher data quality in your new cloud environment.

 

 

Check the blog written by Philipp Kreutzheide

Streamline Your Migration Troubleshooting with the New Transfer List

Transfer List

In any complex data migration project, ensuring data is transferred accurately is paramount. When issues arise during the simulation or migration phases, pinpointing the root cause can be time-consuming. To bring greater transparency to this process, we are introducing the Transfer List for the migration approach "Migrate Data Directly from SAP System." This feature is designed for data migration specialists and project teams who need deep visibility into the data transfer process for effective and efficient troubleshooting.


A Clear View for Faster Resolution

The Transfer List provides a detailed view of the data exactly as it is passed to the target API that creates the records in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
This capability is particularly beneficial when you need to troubleshoot the simulation or migration process. For example, your team can now analyze the Transfer List to definitively determine whether an issue lies with the target API itself or if it is related to a problem in the structure or field mapping. This clarity helps you isolate problems faster, reduce resolution time, and keep your migration project on track.

To use this feature, the simulation or migration process must first be completed for the relevant migration object instance. Once complete, you can access the Transfer List with the following steps:

1. From the Migration Project screen, select the "Instances" link for your object.
2. On the Migration Object Instances screen, select the instance you wish to investigate.
3. Choose the "Transfer List" button.

On the subsequent screen, you can view the target parameters, fields, and their corresponding values that were used in the transfer, providing your team with the precise information needed for analysis. This enhancement empowers you to resolve issues with confidence and precision, ensuring a smoother transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Check the blog written by Philipp Kreutzheide

Increased File Size Limits for an Efficient Data Load

To further streamline your data migration activities, we have increased the file size limits for the "Migrate Data Using Staging Tables" approach. This practical update allows for more efficient handling of large data volumes.

When you upload your data using XML files, you can bundle multiple files into a single zip file for a more convenient upload. With this release, the maximum size for each individual XML file contained within the zip file has been increased to 400MB. This allows you to process larger data sets in a single file. Please be aware that while individual files can be larger, the total size of the zip file itself should not exceed 100MB upon upload to the migration cockpit. This improvement simplifies the data loading process and reduces the number of uploads required for extensive data sets.

I hope you're as excited about these updates as I am! To learn more, I encourage you to explore the SAP Help Portal and join the conversation on the SAP Community.

What is New?

Check Migration Cockpit Community

SAP Help Data Migration

SAP Community YouTube presented by Claudia Gemm and Elizaveta Kozhevenikova

entry.title: PLMSI 3.0 FP04 – What You Need to Know About the Latest Release
entry.content:
Another year has started and we have seen the birth of another feature pack for PLM System Integration for SAP S/4HANA.

Find out what's new and why it matters.

Extended characteristic mapping for Product and Document exchange outbound scenario

Release 3.0 FP04 introduces a more flexible outbound mapping layer for product versions (PV) and document versions (DV).
In addition to class-based characteristic exchange, consultants can now configure:

  • Outbound class assignments, including class numbers and class types, per product version
  • Mappings of class characteristics to target fields in the Domain Model, or to extension fields of the product version
  • Conversion routines, default values, and value mapping tables

This gives solution architects a finer‑grained control mechanism to harmonize master data between SAP and external PLM systems, which proves very useful in multi-system engineering landscapes.

For more information, see Map DM Product Version to SAP Material Master.

Likewise, this feature is available for Document Version Integration. 

For more information, see Map DM Document Version to SAP Document Info Record.

Outbound maintenance requests for classification changes

FP04 adds the ability to trigger outbound maintenance requests for PV as material master, DV as document info record, CO as change request, and CO as engineering change master, based on:

  • Class assignments
  • Characteristic value updates
  • Value changes 

Change Object Integration: In the new view Define Classification Conditions for Outbound in the Customizing for Map DM Change Object to SAP Change Master or Map DM Change Object to SAP Change Record, you can now control which characteristics trigger outbound data transfer to an external PLM system when updated in the SAP system. Specify a Class Number, Class Type, and Characteristic to define outbound-relevant classification conditions.

For more information, see Map DM Change Object to SAP Change Master or Map DM Change Object to SAP Change Record.

The same feature is available for Document Version Integration (see Map DM Document Version to SAP Document Info Record) and for Product Version Integration (see Map DM Product Version to SAP Material Master).

For integration architects, this significantly improves synchronization accuracy and reduces the chances of missed updates for downstream processes.

Product and structure version compatibility support in version-controlled BOMs

 
PLMSI now supports product versioning at both header and item level for version-controlled BOMs.
With the new BOM Header Unlimited Validity checkbox, consultants can enable perpetual header validity (defaulting to Jan 1, 1900), simplifying BOM maintenance and reducing the need for repeated header creation. This is particularly relevant for customers with high-frequency engineering iterations and complex BOM structures.
 

For more information, see Map DM Structure Version to SAP Classic BOM.

 

Transfer of iPPE: PVS from an external PLM system

Feature Pack 04 expands structure integration capabilities by adding support for transferring Integrated Product and Process Engineering (iPPE) – Product Variant Structures (PVS). You can now configure inbound processing for:

  • Access nodes & variants
  • Structure nodes & node relationships
  • Component variants

A new integration pattern (SAP_SVasPPECMPPVS) and several supporting BAdIs provide robust extension points for customizing the mapping logic. 

For more information, see Map DM Structure Version to SAP CMP Product Variant Structure.

What Else is New in PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA?

The release delivers additional BAdIs across POL, SV, and change handling scenarios.
Notably, you can now leverage the MPO Value Control Structure, which boosts flexibility when working with extension fields and custom mappings. For teams implementing tailored integration logic, this significantly improves maintainability and robustness. For detailed information, see our What's New Viewer. Please make sure to select the latest version 3.0 FP04 to filter for the most recent updates.

Expand Your Range

We are happy and proud that another members of our PLM system integration family has released a new versions of its product:

PLM system integration, add-on for 3DEXPERIENCE has published its new version 3.3. Check out the Release Notes.

Business Content

As for each feature pack, we have updated our comprehensive set of business content (BC) sets to help you configure your system with ease. For details, see Initial Configuration.

For a comprehensive list of our new and enhanced features for release 3.0 FP04, check out our What's New Viewer.

All relevant information on PLM system integration is also available via the links below:

  • SAP Help Portal: PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Product Availability Matrix (SAP login required): Matrix for PLM system integration 3.0 for SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Roadmap Explorer: PLM system integration

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up-to-date to our latest enablement assets: linkedin.com/in/annette-garcia-lorenzo

entry.title: Business Partner Master Data in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced digital economy, the integrity, security, and accessibility of master data are paramount to operational excellence. High-quality Business Partner data forms the foundation of countless critical processes, from procurement and sales to finance and compliance. I am pleased to share some of the latest innovations within SAP S/4HANA Cloud that are designed to elevate your business partner master data management strategy.

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

To strengthen security and data integrity, we implement header-level authorization checks and proactive duplicate detection to prevent unauthorized access and ensure high-quality, reliable data from the outset. At the same time, onward replication across multisystem landscapes and conversational AI through Joule enable seamless synchronization and intuitive access to information, driving efficiency, transparency, and faster decision-making across the enterprise.


This post will provide an overview of four significant new features: 
1. Enhanced security through header-level authorization checks.
2. Proactive data quality management with an API-based duplicate check.
3. Streamlined data synchronization via onward replication in multisystem landscapes.
4. Efficient data access using our conversational AI, Joule.

1. Enable Business Partner Header-Level Authorization Checks Based on Data Controller and Purpose

Data privacy and compliance are non-negotiable. To provide more granular control over sensitive information, we have introduced the ability to enable authorization checks at the business partner header level based on the combination of a Data Controller and a Purpose.

Feature Scope:
This feature allows for the assignment of specific data controller and purpose combinations directly to a business partner record. Subsequently, the system can enforce authorization checks based on these assignments.

Key Benefits:
The primary advantage is a significant enhancement in access control. With this feature activated, a user must be explicitly authorized for the specific data controller and purpose assigned to a business partner to gain access to that record. This ensures that only relevant personnel can view or process sensitive data, strengthening your data governance and compliance posture in line with regulations like GDPR.

 

Business partner: assignment of purposes along with data controllers

2. Business Partner A2X OData API Operation for Duplicate Check

Maintaining a clean and duplicate-free master data repository is a constant challenge, especially in integrated environments. To address this proactively, we have extended the capabilities of our core Business Partner OData service.

Feature Scope:
A new operation for performing a duplicate check has been added to the `API_BUSINESS_PARTNER` A2X OData service. This operation can be invoked for Business Partners, Customers, and Suppliers.

Key Benefits:
This new functionality empowers external applications to identify potential duplicates before a new master data record is created via the API. By embedding this check into the creation process, organizations can prevent the introduction of redundant data at the source. This is a crucial step toward retaining high master data quality, reducing downstream cleanup efforts, and ensuring process consistency across the enterprise.

Please refer to following Blogs:

  • Using Business Partner OData API (Saranga)
  • Deep Payload for Business Partner OData API (Mani)

Duplicate Check in Business Partner A2X

 

3. Business Partner Integration: Onward Replication in a Multisystem Landscape

For organizations managing complex system landscapes, ensuring data consistency across all systems is a major undertaking. Our new onward replication capability simplifies this process significantly.

Feature Scope:
This feature allows you to trigger the onward replication of incoming business partner data from your SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP system to all other connected systems in your landscape. Activation is managed through a simple self-service configuration user interface (SSCUI).

Key Benefits:
This innovation enables your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system to function as a true master data hub. When a business partner is created or changed, the system automatically distributes these updates to all subscribed downstream systems. This automated synchronization ensures data consistency across the entire landscape, reduces manual integration efforts, and minimizes the risk of data-related process failures.

Onward Replication of Business Partner Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP

 

Community Blog: Onward Replication of Business Partner master data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

4. Joule as Efficient Access to Business Partner, Customer, and Supplier Master Data

In our pursuit of a more intelligent and user-friendly ERP experience, we have integrated our conversational AI, Joule, with master data management.

Feature Scope:
Joule now allows users to efficiently access attributes of Business Partner, Customer, and Supplier master data objects using natural language queries.

Key Benefits:
This feature fundamentally streamlines the process of data retrieval for business users. Instead of navigating through multiple Fiori apps and screens, a user can simply ask Joule a question, such as "Show me the addresses for Business Partner 100345" or "What are the dunning data details for Customer XYZ?". The key benefits include:

- Enhanced Productivity: Immediate access to necessary data saves time and effort, allowing analysts to focus on decision-making rather than data retrieval.
- Reduced UI Clicks: Joule provides extremely quick information about attributes without requiring users to open dedicated applications.
- Intuitive Interaction: Natural language input makes data access more accessible to a wider range of users.
BP, Customer, Supplier Master Data in SAP Joule

Joule can provide details ranging from a business partner overview, roles, and relationships to specific customer sales area data and supplier purchasing organization details.

 


Community Blog; Exploring Business Partner Master Data with SAP Joule, the AI Co-pilot

These four features represent a significant step forward in making master data management more secure, intelligent, and efficient within SAP S/4HANA Cloud. By enhancing security, improving data quality at the point of entry, simplifying landscape-wide synchronization, and accelerating user access, we are empowering our customers to build a more robust and reliable data foundation for their enterprise.

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

entry.title: Benefits of Maintenance Planning Overview Fiori Application
entry.content:
In the fast-paced world of industrial asset management, the difference between a high-performing plant and a costly bottleneck often comes down to how quickly a maintenance planner can identify and react to issues.

In the legacy SAP environment, this meant digging through multiple transaction codes (T-codes) to find overdue orders or missing parts. With SAP S/4HANA, the Maintenance Planning Overview (App ID: F2828) Fiori application changes the game by consolidating critical data into a single, proactive dashboard.

Here is an overview of why this app is becoming the "command center" for modern maintenance planners.

What is the Maintenance Planning Overview App?

The Maintenance Planning Overview is an analytical Fiori application designed as a "one-stop shop" for Maintenance Planners. Built on the SAP Fiori Overview Page (OVP) floorplan, it uses interactive "cards" to display high-level KPIs and real-time data visualizations.

Instead of searching for problems, the problems are pushed to you. The app analyzes factors like open notifications, missing spare parts, and overdue orders based on a reference period (e.g., "This Week" or "Next Week") that you define.

Key Features: Your Planning Toolkit

The app is organized into task-oriented cards. Each card serves a specific purpose in the maintenance lifecycle:

1. Planning & Backlog Management

  • Orders for Planning: Identifies maintenance orders that have been created but not yet released. It visualizes these by priority or order type using pie charts.

  • Outstanding Notifications: Shows notifications that haven't been assigned to an order yet, allowing you to catch work requests before they fall through the cracks.

2. Material & Procurement Visibility

  • Purchase Requisitions/Orders Not Approved: Tracks non-stock materials required for maintenance. You can see which parts might cause delays because the requisition hasn't been approved or the purchase order hasn't been sent.

  • Non-Stock Materials Not Available: A critical card that flags ordered parts that will not arrive by the required start date of the maintenance order.

3. Execution Tracking

  • Overdue Orders: Highlights released orders where the end date has passed but the operations haven't been finally confirmed.

  • Orders Not Technically Completed: Provides a list of orders that are physically finished but haven't been closed out in the system, which is vital for accurate financial reporting.

 

 

The app typically includes the following standard Fiori cards:

1. Maintenance Order Cards

  • Orders for Planning: Visualizes maintenance orders that have been created but not yet released. It usually breaks them down by Priority or Order Type.

  • Overdue Orders: Analyzes released maintenance orders where the basic end date has already passed, but the operations have not been finally confirmed.

  • Orders for Completion: Specifically highlights orders where all operations are confirmed (CNF) but the order header has not yet been set to Technically Completed (TECO).

  • Jobs (Order Operations): Displays a list of upcoming or current maintenance "jobs" (operations) that require attention or execution within the defined reference period.

2. Maintenance Notification Cards

  • Outstanding Notifications: Displays notifications that have been created but are not yet assigned to a maintenance order. This helps prevent a backlog of unaddressed work requests.

  • Recently Created Notifications: Gives a quick look at the newest requests entering the system, categorized by their processing status.

3. Procurement & Material Cards

  • Non-Stock Materials Not Available: This is a high-value card that flags non-stock components (parts ordered specifically for an order) that are not expected to arrive by the order's start date.

  • Purchase Requisitions Not Approved: Identifies PRs linked to maintenance orders that are stalled in the approval workflow, which could delay planned repairs.

  • Purchase Requisitions Not Converted: Shows approved requisitions that have not yet been turned into formal Purchase Orders (POs).

4. Technical Object Cards

  • Technical Object Damages: Often used to visualize which assets are experiencing the most frequent issues, categorized by damage codes or object parts.

     

Key Features of These Cards

  • Filter Sensitivity: All cards react to the global filters at the top of the app (e.g., Planning Plant, Planner Group, or Time Period).

  • Drill-Down Capability: Clicking the header of a card takes you to a detailed list view (like the "Manage Maintenance Orders" app), while clicking a specific data point (like a slice of a pie chart) filters that list further.

  • Personalization: Users can hide cards they don't need or rearrange them on the dashboard to put the most critical KPIs at the top

entry.title: Validation Messages: CBS and IBS Configuration
entry.content:
As part of the transition to the new CBS (Contribuição sobre Bens e Serviços) and IBS (Imposto sobre Bens e Serviços) tax structure, SAP has introduced three new validation messages when you receive inbound electronic fiscal documents.

Understanding the New Messages 

The messages address common discrepancies that may arise when processing XML documents under Brazil Tax Reform: 

J1B_NFE660: Expected CBS/IBS tax rate is X but tax rate in XML is Y   

This message alerts users when there's a mismatch between the expected tax rate configured in the system and the rate specified in the incoming XML document. 

J1B_NFE661: CBS/IBS tax not available in XML for purchase order item X   

This validation triggers when the required CBS/IBS tax information is missing from the XML for a specific purchase order item. 

J1B_NFE663: Tax group CBS/IBS from XML not available in the current Tax Code   

This message appears when the tax group referenced in the XML document cannot be found in the system's current tax code configuration. 

You can configure each message to appear as either Warning or Error, depending on your business requirements. For detailed instructions on configuring these messages through transaction OBMSG, please refer to Configuring Messages 660, 661, and 663 in OBMSG and OBA5. 

 

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “Like”, share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content.  

You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the  SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the  SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica)  tag and the  SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Community  to keep up with the latest NF-e news.  

Thank you, and until next time,  
Juliana Jarotzky

entry.title: Localization in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
In today's interconnected world, running a global business can feel like navigating a vast ocean. Each country is a new port with its own unique set of rules, regulations, and customs. With the 2602 release, we're not just giving you a better map; we're upgrading your entire navigation system, ensuring you can sail smoothly and confidently, no matter the destination.

Let’s dive into the exciting new features that will help you go global while staying perfectly local.

  • A Single Source of Truth: The Intuitive Tax Register
  • Mastering Global Complexity: Country-Specific Highlights
  • Universal Innovations for Global Business
  • AI campaigns

A Single Source of Truth: The Intuitive Tax Register

Imagine a world where your tax data isn't scattered across different ledgers and reports, but lives in one, centralized, and transparent place. This world is here with the new Tax Register for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. This is a true game changer for tax accountants.

We are providing a single source of truth for all your indirect tax operations. This powerful tool allows you to:

1) Visualize the entire lifecycle of each tax line, from posting to reporting and payment.
2) Gain centralized insights and a real-time view of your tax position, company wide.
3) Increase flexibility by making tax operations independent from accounting.
4) Effortlessly reconcile and ensure you are always audit-ready.

Initially rolling out for VAT in Germany, Belgium, the Netherlands, and Oman, the Tax Register is your new command center for managing tax payables and ensuring comprehensive compliance.

  • Display Tax Items (ID: F4756):
    • View and analyze posted tax items with tax-specific attributes.
    • Track the lifecycle of tax items.
    • Navigate to related documents for detailed insights.

Display Tax Items (ID: F4756)

  • Manage Tax Payable (ID: F4755)
    • Create, post, and cancel tax payable documents.
    • Automatically generate documents when reports reach Content Accepted by Government status.
    • Option to manually create tax payable documents if required.

Manage Tax Payable (ID: F4755)

  • Migrate Tax Items(ID: F6715): Migrate tax items posted prior to Tax Register activation from the standard database table (BSET) to the new table (TAX_REG_ITEM) for reporting and analysis.

 

   Migrate Tax Items (ID: F6715)

Mastering Global Complexity: Country-Specific Highlights

Every region has its own complexities, and our 2602 release delivers targeted solutions to make your life easier.

Turkey: Taming Foreign Exchange Gains

Navigating the nuances of foreign currency in Turkey just got a whole lot simpler. Businesses know the challenge of the (Kur fark) foreign exchange gain invoice required when exchange rates shift between invoicing and payment. Our update automates the creation of billing documents for these gains in Convergent Invoicing. The system automatically calculates the gain, ensures correct VAT treatment, and generates the necessary invoices and accounting documents, saving you invaluable time and minimizing errors.
(Kur fark) foreign exchange gains

Brazil: Unprecedented Flexibility and Extensibility

Brazil's fiscal landscape is famously dynamic, and we're putting more power than ever into your hands. This release introduces a suite of enhancements for Brazil, including:

a. Self-Service for External Tax Engines: A new configuration interface allows you to map custom tax type codes to an external tax engine, enhancing flexibility in sales processing.
b. Extensible Nota Fiscal Models: You can now extend and adapt nota fiscal models for sales processes, allowing you to meet specific requirements not covered by the standard version.
c. Multiple Account Assignments: We now enable external tax calculation for multiple account assignment distribution during invoice verification, enhancing customer satisfaction and accuracy.

Universal Innovations for Global Business

Compliance is a global theme, and some of our most powerful new features are designed to work across borders.

1) Business Partner Certificates (Cross-Country): Managing customer certificates and their validity dates is a critical compliance task. You can now configure and maintain these certificates directly in the business partner master data, making the information readily available for processes like invoicing and payments. This improves compliance and reduces manual effort across all your regions of operation.

Business Partner Certificate

 

2) Expanded Scope Item Availability (Cross-Country): Our global family is always growing! We are thrilled to announce the availability of more scope items for dozens of SAP-delivered standard local versions. From Argentina to Australia, from Canada to Colombia, and from Singapore to South Africa, we are making it easier than ever to steer your business operations globally using SAPs standard, automated, and compliant processes.

AI campaigns

We’re excited to invite you to participate in the beta testing program for innovative new capabilities:

  1.  AI-Assisted Incoming Bills of Exchange Posting. Ready to get involved? Click the Register button at the top left to secure your spot. Together, let's make handling Bills of Exchange more efficient and straightforward!  
  2. EASYFIT: AI-Assisted Easy Entry of Treasury Financial Transactions: Get a preview of how AI can simplify the process of recording financial transactions in Treasury and Risk Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition while ensuring compliance with country specific regulations.

Delta localization information on new, changed or deleted features as well as legal changes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 for your country? Check the Help documentation country/region specific section What’s new

Check Localization Community

The 2602 release for Localization is more than just a list of features; it's our steadfast commitment to helping you navigate the complexities of global commerce with confidence and ease. We handle the local complexities so you can focus on what you do best: grow your business.

I hope you're as excited about these updates as I am! To learn more, I encourage you to explore the SAP Help Portal and join the conversation on the SAP Community.

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Localization as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.
